title: Finite Rounding Shadow Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite exact-vs-rounded arithmetic bad equality QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational replay computes exact_delta = 1/10000 while a fixed three-decimal rounding grid computes rounded_delta_after_sum = 0, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional rounding-shadow rows only when they add distinct rounding-mode, cancellation, accumulation, interval-error, QF_FP, bit-vector, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep IEEE semantics and stability theorems in future QF_FP or Lean-horizon lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational addition, exact increment, three-decimal rounding grid, rounded increment, and exact-vs-rounded difference as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false exact-vs-rounded equality through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes both scalar values.
Expose the pack through real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and exact-vs-floating learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep IEEE floating-point semantics, hardware rounding modes, accumulation-error theory, and numerical stability marked as future QF_FP, bit-vector, numerical-honesty, or Lean-horizon work until formal routes exist.
